hooks.prompt:
Review the code change for testing quality issues. Check for:
1. Testing implementation instead of behavior — tests that assert internal state, private methods, or implementation details instead of observable outputs
2. Missing act() wrapper — React state updates in tests without act() cause warnings and flaky results
3. Hardcoded waits (setTimeout, sleep) — use waitFor, findBy, or poll-based assertions instead of fixed delays
4. No cleanup between tests — shared state, DOM nodes, or mocks leaking between test cases
5. Overly broad assertions — toMatchObject on entire response when only one field matters, or snapshot tests on large volatile objects
6. Missing error case tests — only testing the happy path without testing validation errors, network failures, or edge cases
7. Test data coupling — tests depending on execution order, database state from other tests, or shared mutable fixtures
8. Mocking too much — mocking the thing you're testing, or mocking so much that the test proves nothing
9. Missing async assertions — not awaiting async operations, causing tests to pass before assertions run
10. No accessibility queries — using getByTestId when getByRole, getByLabelText, or getByText would be more meaningful

Respond {"ok": true} if no issues found, or {"ok": false, "reason": "specific issue description"} if problems detected.
